Contraception","faqs":[{"question":"What is emergency contraception?","answer":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eEmergency contraception, often referred to as the morning-after pill, is a form of birth control used to prevent pregnancy after unprotected sex or contraceptive failure.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n"},{"question":"When should I consider taking emergency contraception?","answer":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eWe recommend using emergency contraception as soon as possible after unprotected sex, ideally within 72 hours, although certain options (such as \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"/products/ella/\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e\u003cu\u003eElla®\u003c/u\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e) may be effective up to 120 hours afterward.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n"},{"question":"How does emergency contraception work?","answer":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eEmergency contraception primarily works by preventing or delaying ovulation, inhibiting fertilization, or interfering with the implantation of a fertilized egg. It does not terminate an existing pregnancy.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n"},{"question":"Is a prescription required for emergency contraception?","answer":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eWhile some forms of emergency contraception are available over the counter without a prescription (like \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"/products/econtra-one-step/\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e\u003cu\u003eEContra One-Step\u003c/u\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e or Plan B), others may require a prescription from a healthcare provider (like \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca href=\"/products/ella/\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e\u003cu\u003eElla®\u003c/u\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003e).\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n"},{"question":"Are there age restrictions for purchasing emergency contraception?","answer":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eRegulations vary by location. In some places, there may be age restrictions for purchasing certain types of emergency contraception over the counter. It\u0026#8217;s essential to be aware of local laws and policies.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n"},{"question":"How effective is emergency contraception?","answer":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eEffectiveness varies depending on the type of emergency contraception used and how quickly it is taken after unprotected sex. Generally, the sooner it\u0026#8217;s taken, the more effective it is in preventing pregnancy.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n"},{"question":"How quickly can I get emergency contraception?","answer":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan style=\"font-weight: 400;\"\u003eIf you need emergency contraception immediately, we offer overnight shipping. Title\u003c/u\u003e","information_description":"\u003csmall\u003e\u003cb\u003eWARNING: CARDIOVASCULAR DISORDERS, PROBABLE DEMENTIA, BREAST CANCER, and ENDOMETRIAL CANCER\u003c/b\u003e \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003ci\u003eSee full prescribing information for complete boxed warning.\u003c/i\u003e \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cu\u003eEstrogen Plus Progestin Therapy\u003c/u\u003e \u003cul style=\"text-align:left\"\u003e\u003cli\u003eThe Women’s Health Initiative (WHI) estrogen plus progestin substudy reported increased risks of pulmonary embolism (PE), deep vein thrombosis (DVT), stroke, and myocardial infarction (MI).\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eThe WHI estrogen plus progestin substudy reported increased risks of invasive breast cancer.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eThe WHI Memory Study (WHIMS) estrogen plus progestin ancillary study of WHI reported an increased risk of probable dementia in postmenopausal women 65 years of age and older.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eDo not use estrogen plus progestogen therapy for the prevention of cardiovascular disease or dementia.\u003c/li\u003e \u003c/ul\u003e \u003cu\u003eEstrogen-Alone Therapy\u003c/u\u003e \u003cul style=\"text-align:left\"\u003e   \u003cli\u003eThere is an increased risk of endometrial cancer in a woman with a uterus who uses unopposed estrogens.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eThe WHI estrogen-alone sub study reported increased risks of stroke and DVT.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eThe WHI Memory Study (WHIMS) estrogen plus progestin ancillary study of WHI reported an increased risk of probable dementia in postmenopausal women 65 years of age and older.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eDo not use estrogen-alone therapy for the prevention of cardiovascular disease or dementia.\u003c/li\u003e \u003c/ul\u003e \u003cdiv style=\"text-align:left\"\u003eThe WHI estrogen-alone and estrogen plus progestin sub studies evaluated only daily oral conjugated estrogens (CE) [0.625 mg] and medroxyprogesterone acetate (MPA) [2.5 mg]. Therefore, the relevance of the WHI findings regarding adverse cardiovascular events, dementia, and breast cancer to lower CE and MPA doses, other routes of administration, or other estrogen-alone or estrogen plus progestogen products is not known and cannot be definitively excluded or determined.\u003c/div\u003e \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cb\u003eMost important information you should know about BIJUVA (a combination of estrogen and progestogen):\u003c/b\u003e \u003cul style=\"text-align:left\"\u003e   \u003cli\u003eDo not use estrogens with or without progestogens to prevent heart disease, heart attacks, strokes, or dementia (decline of brain function).\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eUsing estrogens with progestogens may increase your chances of getting heart attacks, strokes, breast cancer, or blood clots.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eUsing estrogens with progestogens may increase your chance of getting dementia, based on a study of women 65 years of age and older.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eUsing estrogen-alone may increase your chance of getting cancer of the uterus.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eDo not use estrogen-alone to prevent heart disease, heart attacks, strokes, or dementia (decline of brain function).\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eUsing estrogen-alone may increase your chances of getting strokes or blood clots.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eUsing estrogen-alone may increase your chance of getting dementia, based on a study of women 65 years of age and older.\u003c/li\u003e \u003c/ul\u003e You and your healthcare provider should talk regularly about whether you still need treatment with BIJUVA.  \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cb\u003eDO NOT USE BIJUVA IF YOU:\u003c/b\u003e \u003cul style=\"text-align:left\"\u003e   \u003cli\u003eHave had your uterus (womb) removed (hysterectomy). BIJUVA contains a progestogen to decrease the chance of getting cancer of the uterus. If you do not have a uterus, you do not need a progestogen and you should not use BIJUVA.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHave any unusual vaginal bleeding. Vaginal bleeding after menopause may be a warning sign of cancer of the uterus (womb). Your healthcare provider should check any unusual vaginal bleeding to find out the cause.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHave been diagnosed with a bleeding disorder.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHave or have had certain cancers. Estrogens may increase the chances of getting certain types of cancers, including cancer of the breast or uterus (womb). If you have or have had cancer, talk with your healthcare provider about whether you should use BIJUVA.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHave or have had blood clots.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHad a stroke or heart attack.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHave or have had liver problems.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eAre allergic to BIJUVA or any of its ingredients.\u003c/li\u003e \u003c/ul\u003e \u003cb\u003e\u003cbr\u003eWARNINGS\u003c/b\u003e \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003eAsk a doctor before use if you: \u003cul style=\"text-align:left\"\u003e   \u003cli\u003eHave high levels of fat in your blood (triglycerides).\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHave any unusual vaginal bleeding.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHave any other medical conditions that may become worse while you are using BIJUVA, such as: asthma (wheezing); diabetes; a genetic problem called porphyria; lupus; hypertension (high blood pressure); have high calcium in your blood; epilepsy (seizures); migraine; endometriosis; angioedema (swelling of face or tongue); problems with your heart, liver, thyroid or kidneys.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eAre going to have surgery or will be on bed rest.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eAre pregnant or think you may be pregnant. BIJUVA is not for pregnant women.\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eAre breastfeeding.\u003c/li\u003e \u003c/ul\u003e Tell your doctor or pharmacist about all the medicines you take, including prescription and over-the-counter medicines, vitamins, and herbal supplements. \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cb\u003eWHEN USING BIJUVA, YOU MAY HAVE\u003c/b\u003e \u003cul style=\"text-align:left\"\u003e   \u003cli\u003eBreast tenderness\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eHeadache\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eNausea\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eVaginal bleeding\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eVaginal discharge\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003ePelvic pain\u003c/li\u003e \u003c/ul\u003e Call your doctor right away if you get any of the following warning signs: \u003cul style=\"text-align:left\"\u003e   \u003cli\u003eNew breast lumps\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eUnusual vaginal bleeding\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eChanges in vision or speech\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eSudden new severe headaches\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eSevere pains in your chest or legs with or without shortness of breath, weakness, and fatigue\u003c/li\u003e \u003cli\u003eVomiting\u003c/li\u003e \u003c/ul\u003e \u003cb\u003eUSE\u003c/b\u003e \u003cbr\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cdiv style=\"text-align:left\"\u003eBIJUVA is a prescription medicine that contains two hormones, an estrogen, and a progestogen. BIJUVA is used after menopause to reduce moderate to severe hot flashes in women with a uterus.\u003c/div\u003e \u003cbr\u003e\u003cdiv style=\"text-align:left\"\u003eThe risk information provided here is not complete. To learn more, review the BIJUVA Patient Information and talk about BIJUVA with your healthcare provider or pharmacist. The FDA-approved product labeling, including Patient Information, can be found \u003ca href=\"https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/fda/fdaDrugXsl.cfm?setid=bc52489a-5149-411d-9665-2c75405ad15d\u0026type=display\"\u003ehere\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/div\u003e \u003cbr\u003e\u003cdiv style=\"text-align:left\"\u003eYou are encouraged to report negative side effects of prescription drugs to the FDA. Visit \u003ca href=\"https://www.fda.gov/safety/medwatch-fda-safety-information-and-adverse-event-reporting-program\"\u003eMedWatch\u003c/a\u003e or call 1-800-FDA-1088. \u003c/div\u003e\u003c/small\u003e","information_list":[{"category":"Information List Category 1","content":"\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBlandit libero volutpat sed cras ornare arcu dui vivamus.
